We and our 641 IAB TCF partners store and access information on your device for the following purposes: store and/or access information on a device, advertising and content measurement, audience research, and services development, personalised advertising, and personalised content.

Personal data may be processed to do the following: use precise geolocation data and actively scan device characteristics for identification.

Our third party IAB TCF partners may store and access information on your device such as IP address and device characteristics. Our IAB TCF Partners may process this personal data on the basis of legitimate interest, or with your consent. You may change or withdraw your preferences for this website at any time by clicking on the cookie icon or link; however, as a consequence, you may not see relevant ads or personalised content. You may change your settings at any time or accept the default settings. You may close this banner to continue with only essential cookies. Privacy Policy

George Springer

Position: Rightfielder

Bats: Right  •  Throws: Right

6-1, 220lb (185cm, 99kg)

Team: Toronto Blue Jays (majors)

Born: September 19, 1989 (Age: 36-241d) in New Britain, CT us

Draft: Drafted by the Minnesota Twins in the 48th round of the 2008 MLB June Amateur Draft from Avon Old Farms School (Avon, CT) and the Houston Astros in the 1st round (11th) of the 2011 MLB June Amateur Draft from University of Connecticut (Storrs, CT).

High School: Avon Old Farms School (Avon, CT)

School: University of Connecticut (Storrs, CT)

Debut: April 16, 2014 (Age 24-209d, 20,586th in major league history)
   vs. KCR 5 AB, 1 H, 0 HR, 0 RBI, 0 SB

Rookie Status: Exceeded rookie limits during 2014 season

2026 Contract Status: Signed thru 2026, 6 yrs/$150M (21-26)

Service Time (01/2026): 11.166  •  Free Agent: 2027

Agents: Excel Sports Management • Previously: The Legacy Agency

National Team: us USA (College)

Full Name: George Chelston Springer

Nicknames: Horhay

View Player Info from the B-R Bullpen

  • 4x All-Star
  • 2017 World Series
  • 3x Silver Slugger
  • WS MVP

Wins Above Replacement

<strong>Wins Above Replacement</strong><br>A single number that presents the number of wins the player added<br>to the team above what a replacement player (think AAA or AAAA) would add.<br>Scale for a single-season: 8+ MVP Quality, 5+ All-Star Quality, 2+ Starter,<br>0-2 Reserve, < 0 Replacement Level <br>Developed by Sean Smith of BaseballProjection.com

2019 AL  6.5 (10th)
2020 AL  2.2 (10th)
Active  41.8 (30th)

WAR Position Players

<strong>Wins Above Replacement for position players</strong><br>A single number that presents the number of wins the player added<br>to the team above what a replacement player (think AAA or AAAA) would add.<br>Scale: 8+ MVP Quality, 5+ All-Star Quality, 2+ Starter,<br>0-2 Reserve, < 0 Replacement Level <br> Developed by Sean Smith of BaseballProjection.com

2019 AL  6.5 (6th)
2020 AL  2.2 (7th)
Active  41.8 (23rd)

Offensive WAR

<strong>Offensive Wins Above Replacement (everything but Fielding)</strong><br>The same statistic as Wins Above Replacement for Position<br>Players (WAR), but with the fielding value excluded.<br>oWAR + dWAR does not equal WAR.<br>Adding would count positions twice.<br>Contains the factor for batting stats, baserunning, a positional adjustment, and the replacement player adjustment.<br> Factors developed by Sean Smith of BaseballProjection.com

2017 AL  4.9 (9th)
2019 AL  5.1 (9th)
2025 AL  5.5 (7th)
Active  39.7 (22nd)

<strong>Hits/At Bats</strong><br>For recent years, leaders need 3.1 PA<br>per team game played<br>Bold indicates either highest BA using current stats<br>or awarded title at end of year.

<strong>(H + BB + HBP)/(At Bats + BB + HBP + SF)</strong><br>For recent years, leaders need 3.1 PA<br>per team game played

Adjusted OPS+

<strong>OPS+</strong><br>100*[OBP/lg OBP + SLG/lg SLG - 1]<br>Adjusted to the player&#x2019;s ballpark(s)

Runs Created

<strong>Runs Created</strong><br>A set of formulas developed by Bill James and others<br>that estimates a player&#x2019;s total contributions<br>to a team&#x2019;s runs total.<br>This is computed with the "technical" formula when possible.<br>If SB or CS data is missing, the "basic" formula is used.<br>If HBP, IBB, SH, SF, or GIDP data is missing, the "stolen base" version of the formula is used. <br>Team Runs Created is the sum of player Runs Created.

2025 AL  116 (3rd)
Active  976 (17th)

Adj. Batting Runs

<strong>Adjusted Batting Runs</strong><br>A set of formulas developed by Gary Gillette, Pete Palmer and others<br>that estimates a player&#x2019;s total contributions<br>to a team&#x2019;s runs total via linear weights.<br>0.0 is an avg performance, <0 is worse than avg and >0 is better than avg

2017 AL  33 (8th)
2019 AL  35 (6th)
2025 AL  43 (3rd)
Active  213 (19th)

Adj. Batting Wins

<strong>Adjusted Batting Wins</strong><br>A set of formulas developed by Gary Gillette, Pete Palmer and others<br>that estimates a player&#x2019;s total contributions<br>to a team&#x2019;s wins with his bat.<br>0.0 is an avg performance, <0 is worse than avg and >0 is better than avg

2017 AL  3.2 (8th)
2019 AL  3.3 (6th)
2025 AL  4.2 (3rd)
Active  20.6 (19th)

Times On Base

<strong>Times on Base</strong><br>Hits + Bases on Balls + Hit By Pitch<br>Does not include Reached on Error or Catcher Interference.

2016 AL  267 (4th)
2025 AL  231 (10th)
Active  2,272 (18th)

Offensive Win %

<strong>Offensive Winning Percentage</strong><br>The percentage of games a team with nine of this player<br>batting would win. Assumes average pitching and defense.<br>This uses the Pythagorean win pct formula with the player's RC/G for runs scored and the league's R/9 as runs allowed.

2019 AL  .713 (4th)
2025 AL  .766 (2nd)
Active  .621 (30th)

Double Plays Grounded Into

Power-Speed #

<strong>Power/Speed Number</strong><br>2 x (Home Runs x Stolen Bases)/(Stolen Bases + Home Runs)<br>The harmonic mean of HR and SB.<br>To do well you need a lot of both.<br>Developed by Bill James.

2015 AL  16.0 (10th)

Base-Out Runs Added (RE24)

<strong>Base-Out Runs Added</strong><br>Given the bases occupied/out situation, how many runs did the batter<br> or baserunner add in the resulting play.<br>Compared to average, so 0 is average, and above 0 is better than average

2025 AL  41.05 (4th)
Active  201.17 (19th)

Win Probability Added (WPA)

<strong>Win Probability Added for Offensive Player</strong><br>Given average teams, this is the change in probability<br>caused by this batter during the game.<br>A change of +/- 1 would indicate one win added or lost.

2017 AL  2.9 (10th)
2025 AL  3.2 (8th)
Active  14.3 (28th)

Situ. Wins Added (WPA/LI)

<strong>Situational Wins (WPA/LI)</strong><br>It is the sum of WPA divided by the leverage index for each play.<br>WPA depends greatly on the context of the at bats.<br>This stat does not.

2025 AL  4.4 (3rd)
Active  18.9 (20th)

Championship WPA (cWPA)

<strong>Championship Win Probability Added for Offensive Player</strong><br>Given average teams, this is the change in probability, displayed in percentage points.<br>A change of +/- 100% would indicate one world series win added or lost.

2015 AL  2.5 (5th)
2020 AL  0.8 (8th)
2022 AL  1.5 (8th)
2025 AL  2.5 (6th)
Active  10.1 (18th)

Base-Out Wins Added (REW)

<strong>Base-Out Wins Added (REW)</strong><br>This is the number of wins above average the player was worth by their performance measured by the 24 <br>base-out situations across every play in the game.

Total Zone Runs

<strong>Total Zone Total Fielding Runs Above Avg</strong><br>The number of runs above or below average the player was worth based on the number of plays made.<br>This number combines the R<em>tz</em>, R<em>dp</em>, R<em>of</em>, R<em>catch</em> numbers into a total defensive contribution.<br>See the <a href="/about/total_zone.shtml">glossary section</a> for a more complete explanation.<br><em>Provided by BaseballProjection.com</em>

Transactions

June 5, 2008: Drafted by the Minnesota Twins in the 48th round of the 2008 amateur draft, but did not sign.

June 6, 2011: Drafted by the Houston Astros in the 1st round (11th pick) of the 2011 amateur draft. Player signed August 15, 2011.

October 28, 2020: Granted Free Agency.

January 23, 2021: Signed as a Free Agent with the Toronto Blue Jays.

The transaction information used here was obtained free of charge from and is copyrighted by Retrosheet. We attempt to update transactions throughout the season.

Name + "Statistics" Translations

  • Note, this is done in an automated way, so we apologize for any errors, & please report any suggested corrections. "Statistics" is included to allow non-English speakers to find our pages.

French: George Springer statistiques

Japanese: ジョージ・スプリンガー 成績

Polish: George Springer Statystyki

Frequently Asked Questions

How old is George Springer?

George Springer is 36 years old.

When was George Springer born?

George Springer was born on September 19, 1989.

Where was George Springer born?

George Springer was born in New Britain, CT.

How tall is George Springer?

George Springer is 6-1 (185 cm) tall.

How much does George Springer weigh?

George Springer weighs 220 lbs (99 kg).

How many seasons has George Springer played?

George Springer is in his 13th season.

When was George Springer drafted?

George Springer was drafted by the Minnesota Twins in the 48th round of the 2008 MLB June Amateur Draft from Avon Old Farms School (Avon, CT) and the Houston Astros in the 1st round (11th) of the 2011 MLB June Amateur Draft from University of Connecticut (Storrs, CT).

What position does George Springer play?

George Springer is a Rightfielder.

How many hits does George Springer have?

George Springer has 19 hits this season and has 1,499 hits over his career.

How many home runs does George Springer have?

George Springer has 2 home runs this season and has 295 home runs over his career.

What is George Springer's average?

George Springer has a .186 average this season and has a .265 average over his career.

How much is George Springer worth?

George Springer makes $24,166,667.00 this year. His current contract terms are 6 yrs/$150M (21-26).

How many teams has George Springer played for?

George Springer has played for 2 teams; the Houston Astros and Toronto Blue Jays.

How many World Series has George Springer won?

George Springer has won 1 World Series.

What are George Springer's nicknames?

Horhay is a nickname for George Springer.
